chapter_title.ar: باب صَلَاةِ الشَّيْخِ الْكَبِيرِ وَ الْمَرِيضِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 64 – The Salāt of an elderly man and the sick

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/335)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/335)
text: اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ الَّذِينَ يَذْكُرُونَ اللَّهَ قِياماً وَ قُعُوداً وَ عَلى جُنُوبِهِمْ قَالَ الصَّحِيحُ يُصَلِّي قَائِماً وَ قُعُوداً الْمَرِيضُ يُصَلِّي جَالِساً وَ عَلى جُنُوبِهِمْ الَّذِي يَكُونُ أَضْعَفَ مِنَ الْمَرِيضِ الَّذِي يُصَلِّي جَالِساً .

en.hubeali: Ali, from his father, from Ibn Mahboub, from Abu Hamza,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’farasws regarding the Words of Allahazwj Mighty and Majestic [3:191] Those who remember Allah standing and sitting and lying on their sides. Heasws said: ‘The healthy would pray Salāt standing, ‘and sitting’ are the sick praying Salāt seated, and ‘lying on their sides’ are those who happen to be weaker than the sick who pray Salāt seated’.
en.sarwar: 11. Ali has narrated from his father from ibn Mahbub from abu Hamzah who has said the following: “About the words of Allah, most Majestic, most Glorious, ‘. . . those who speak of Allah, standing, sitting and while lying on their sides. . .’ [32:15] abu Ja‘far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘A healthy person performs Salah (prayer) standing. One suffering from an illness performs Salah (prayer) sitting, and ‘on their sides’ is for one who is weaker than a patient who performs Salah (prayer) in a sitting position.’”
